hypothesis: Incorporating a hierarchical reward function in reinforcement learning that explicitly penalizes structural similarity between generated molecules and the pre-training corpus (e.g., drug-like molecules from ChEMBL) while simultaneously rewarding material-specific criteria will produce a greater proportion of CTF candidates passing expert filters than the additive reward averaging currently employed, because directly repelling the model from the drug-like chemical subspace will accelerate navigation toward the sparse, high-reward region of the materials chemical space and reduce the exploitation of drug-like scaffolds that satisfy individual sub-criteria but fail holistic material constraints.
